name: persona
description: Semantic profile for arbitrary custom personas the lexical engine can't capture (tone, role, in-character voice). Scored by an LLM judge against a rubric rather than by token classes. Requires the judge backend to be enabled; falls back to the lexical baseline when unavailable.

rubric: Does the reply stay in the persona/voice the user established?
Does it honor explicit output constraints (length, format, tone)?
Has it relapsed into generic default-assistant phrasing or framing?
judge.scale: 0 = perfectly in-character, 100 = fully reverted to default voice
